EnableEffectForge.Name: Enable Effect Forge interface
EnableEffectForge.Hint: Shows access to Effect Forge in the Items Directory. The Effect Engine and its API remain available.

EnableCriticalForge.Name: Enable Critical Forge
EnableCriticalForge.Hint: Enables the future generation of critical-hit and fumble results in chat.

EffectForge.Title: Effect Forge
EffectForge.Intro: The Effect Engine is active. This initial interface is used to verify the architecture and API.
EffectForge.CompileSuccess: The example definition was translated into a PF2e Effect Item source.
EffectForge.TestIntro: Rudimentary test interface for the Effect Engine. The fields produce a real Effect Definition.
EffectForge.Name: Name
EffectForge.ConsoleHint: For diagnostics, the definition and compiled PF2e Item are also written to the browser console.
EffectForge.ComponentIntro: Component-based test interface for the Effect Engine.
EffectForge.ComponentsHint: Add any number of components and edit them independently.
EffectForge.PreviewEmpty: The Effect Definition appears here after validation.
EffectForge.CompiledEmpty: The PF2e Item appears here after compilation.
EffectForge.ImagePickerUnavailable: The file picker is unavailable in this Foundry version.
EffectForge.CustomSelectorHint: For specialized PF2e or module selectors. Verify the value with “Inspect Roll”.
EffectForge.RecoveryDCHint: Leave empty to use the regular PF2e recovery DC.
EffectForge.FastHealingHint: The target regains this amount at the beginning of its turn.
EffectForge.RegenerationHint: The target regenerates this amount at the beginning of its turn.
EffectForge.RegenerationDeactivatedByHint: Select at least one damage type. Use Ctrl or Cmd for multiple selections.
EffectForge.TemporaryHitPointsHint: Granted once when the effect is applied. Temporary Hit Points normally do not stack.
EffectForge.MovementHint: Positive values increase and negative values reduce the Speed. This component does not grant a movement type the actor does not already have.
EffectForge.BaseSpeedHint: Grants climb, burrow, fly, or swim as a new base Speed. Existing movement modifiers can then alter it.

Notifications.GmOnly: Effect Forge is available to Gamemasters only.
Notifications.NotImplemented: This function is not implemented in architecture release 0.1.0-dev.

Errors.DuplicateComponent: The component type is already registered: {type}

ShakenNerves.Name: Shaken Nerves
ShakenNerves.Description: The target is frightened and mentally vulnerable.
ShakenNerves.PlainDescription: The target is frightened and mentally vulnerable.

Schema.DefinitionObject: The Effect Definition must be an object.
Schema.DurationValue: Duration value must be a non-negative number.
Schema.ComponentsRequired: The effect requires at least one component.
Schema.ComponentObject: The component must be an object.

Rules.FrightenedStatus: Frightened {frightenedValue} already applies a status penalty to this roll. The additional status penalty {modifierValue} normally has no effect while Frightened is at least as high.
Rules.FrightenedCircumstance: The circumstance penalty {modifierValue} can stack with the status penalty from Frightened {frightenedValue}.
Rules.SelectorFormat: Selector “{selector}” is invalid. Selectors must be lowercase kebab-case strings.
Rules.SelectorCustom: “{selector}” is a custom selector. Verify its behavior with “Inspect Roll”.
Rules.ConditionValueIgnored: The condition “{slug}” has no condition value. The supplied value {value} is ignored during compilation.
Rules.PersistentDamageDuplicateType: Multiple components deal persistent {damageType} damage. Instances of the same type normally do not stack; usually only the stronger one matters.
Rules.ResistanceDuplicateType: Multiple components grant resistance to “{resistanceType}”. Resistances of the same type normally do not add together; usually only the highest value matters.
Rules.WeaknessDuplicateType: Multiple components grant weakness to “{weaknessType}”. Weaknesses of the same type normally do not add together; usually only the highest value matters.
Rules.ImmunityDuplicateType: Multiple components grant immunity to “{immunityType}”. The repeated entry is redundant.
Rules.FastHealingMultipleSources: Multiple components grant fast healing. Verify how the sources should interact on the target; in many cases only the highest value is intended.
Rules.RegenerationMultipleSources: Multiple components grant regeneration. Verify their values and deactivation conditions; in many cases only one regeneration source is intended.
Rules.TemporaryHitPointsMultipleSources: Multiple components grant temporary Hit Points. They normally do not stack; compare component {firstComponent} ({firstValue}) with this value ({value}).
Rules.MovementOverlap: Component {firstComponent} and this component affect the same Speed with the same modifier type. Under the stacking rules, normally only the stronger bonus or penalty applies.
Rules.BaseSpeedDuplicateType: Multiple components grant the same movement mode “{movementType}”. Usually only one base Speed of that type should be defined.

Compatibility.TargetPresent: Target analysis was prepared for “{targetName}”. Concrete immunity checks will follow in a later version.

PersistentDamage.FormulaMissing: Persistent damage requires a damage formula, such as 1d6.
PersistentDamage.TypeInvalid: “{damageType}” is not a known PF2e damage type.
PersistentDamage.DcInvalid: The recovery DC must be empty or a positive integer.

Resistance.TypeInvalid: “{resistanceType}” is not a known PF2e resistance type.
Resistance.ValueInvalid: The resistance value must be a positive integer.

Weakness.TypeInvalid: “{weaknessType}” is not a known PF2e weakness type.
Weakness.ValueInvalid: The weakness value must be a positive integer.

Immunity.TypeInvalid: “{immunityType}” is not a known PF2e immunity type.

FastHealing.ValueInvalid: The fast healing value must be a positive integer.

Regeneration.ValueInvalid: The regeneration value must be a positive integer.
Regeneration.DeactivationRequired: Regeneration requires at least one damage type that deactivates it.
Regeneration.TypeInvalid: “{damageType}” is not a known PF2e damage type and cannot deactivate regeneration.
Regeneration.DuplicateType: Damage type “{damageType}” is listed more than once as a deactivation condition.

TemporaryHitPoints.ValueInvalid: The temporary Hit Points value must be a positive integer. Received: {value}.

Movement.TypeInvalid: “{movementType}” is not a supported movement type.
Movement.ValueInvalid: The movement value must be a non-zero integer.
Movement.ModifierTypeInvalid: “{modifierType}” is not a supported movement modifier type.
Movement.UnusualIncrement: The movement change {value} does not use the usual 5-foot increment.

BaseSpeed.TypeInvalid: “{movementType}” cannot be granted as a base Speed.
BaseSpeed.ValueInvalid: The base Speed must be a positive integer.
BaseSpeed.UnusualIncrement: The base Speed {value} does not use the usual 5-foot increment.
